instructions.name: claim
docs: Pays from the shared pool vault, not a per-product vault, and releases this
buyer's share of `owed` on the pool — keeping the pool-wide invariant exact as
claims happen one at a time, in any order, by any number of buyers.

instructions.name: create_product
docs: Creator's writer pool reserves this product's worst-case obligation instead of
moving fresh collateral — the capital was already posted via fund_pool, possibly
backing other open products from the same writer too. This is the cross-product
capital efficiency: one pool, many products, one solvency check, not N separate
dedicated vaults.

instructions.name: finalize_product
docs: Tallies proven legs into the tier table. Legs left unsettled past settle_deadline
default to False so the product always resolves to a payout. Also moves this
product's obligation from the pool's `reserved` (worst case, while open) to `owed`
(the actual confirmed liability) — a pure transfer between the two counters, so the
pool's solvency invariant (vault_balance >= reserved + owed) is unaffected by this
instruction; it only ever changes which bucket an amount sits in, never the total.

instructions.name: fund_pool
docs: Writer adds capital ahead of creating products. Pure balance increase — trivially
preserves the solvency invariant (both sides of `>=` keep their values; only the
left side grows).

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: One-time setup. Stores which program `settle_leg` CPIs into, so a future TxLINE
program migration (e.g. validate_stat_v2 under a new program id) doesn't require
redeploying Strata — just calling update_config again.

instructions.name: initialize_writer_pool
docs: One pool per writer, shared across every product they create. Solvency is a single
running invariant, never a per-product calculation: at all times,
pool_vault_balance >= reserved (open products' worst cases) + owed (settled,
confirmed, not-yet-claimed payouts)
Every instruction below that touches reserved/owed preserves this invariant by
construction — see each one's doc comment for the specific argument.

instructions.name: settle_geo_product
docs: Permissionless, single-shot: settle and finalize together, since a geometric product
resolves to one atomic true/false, not a per-leg tally needing a separate finalize pass.

instructions.name: settle_leg
docs: Permissionless. Caller supplies only proof material; the program constructs the
TraderPredicate from Product.legs[leg_index] and binds the proof to this fixture.

instructions.name: withdraw_from_pool
docs: Writer withdraws idle capital. Only ever allows taking out what's neither reserved
for an open product's worst case nor owed to a settled product's buyers — provably
safe across the writer's ENTIRE book of products at once, not product-by-product.

errors.name: TiersNotMonotonic
errors.msg: tiers must be strictly increasing in legs and non-decreasing in payout

errors.name: MissingZeroTier
errors.msg: must include a tier for 0 legs true

errors.name: InsufficientPoolBalance
errors.msg: withdrawal would dip into reserved or owed pool capital

errors.name: TooEarlyToSettle
errors.msg: too early to settle, wait for closes_at

errors.name: BatchPredatesClose
errors.msg: batch predates closes_at — can't settle on data that existed before deposits closed

errors.name: MissingSecondStat
errors.msg: leg requires a second stat term

errors.name: LegsStillPending
errors.msg: legs still pending and before settle_deadline

errors.name: WrongStatCount
errors.msg: expected exactly 2 stats for a geometric product

types.name: GeoProduct
docs: A geometric (exact-outcome) product, settled via validate_stat_v2's distance predicate
instead of the tiered engine's per-leg validate_stat calls. Binary payout: the predicted
vector either lands within distance_threshold of the real outcome, or it doesn't.
